crops.name: Maize (Corn)

forest.notes: Two cropping seasons possible - early rains (March-April) and late rains (August-September)

savanna.notes: Single cropping season aligned with main rains

sahel.notes: Short window due to limited rainfall, drought-tolerant varieties recommended

soil.testing_tip: Take soil to a depth of 20cm from 5 spots in the field, mix together, and send a sample to a local agricultural extension office for pH and nutrient testing. A cheap DIY check: mix soil with water to form mud, if it holds shape like clay it's too heavy for maize without amendment.

fertilizer.basal: NPK 15:15:15 at planting, about 100kg per hectare
fertilizer.topdress: Urea (46% N) applied 4-6 weeks after planting when plants are knee-high, about 50kg per hectare
fertilizer.organic_alternative: Well-rotted poultry or cow manure worked into soil 2 weeks before planting, roughly 2-3 handfuls per planting hole

watering.frequency: Rain-fed in most zones; if irrigating, water every 3-4 days during dry spells
watering.critical_stages: Most water-sensitive during tasseling and silking (about 50-60 days after planting) - drought here drastically cuts yield

common_pests_diseases.name: Fall armyworm
common_pests_diseases.remedy: Hand-pick larvae early morning, apply neem extract or recommended insecticide

common_pests_diseases.name: Maize streak virus
common_pests_diseases.remedy: Use resistant varieties, control leafhopper vectors early

common_pests_diseases.name: Stalk borer
common_pests_diseases.remedy: Remove and destroy affected stalks, rotate crops

harvest_timeline.signs_of_readiness: Husks turn pale yellow/brown and dry, kernels hard when pressed with thumbnail
harvest_timeline.harvest_method: Snap cobs off the stalk by hand, dry in sun for 3-5 days before shelling and storage

beginner_tips: Plant after the first reliable rains, not just after one light shower - a false start can mean replanting
Two seeds per hole gives insurance against poor germination, thin to one strong seedling after 2 weeks
Intercropping with cowpea or groundnut helps fix nitrogen in soil and gives you a second harvest

crops.name: Upland Rice

forest.notes: Plant with onset of rains for upland (non-flooded) rice

sahel.notes: Only short-duration varieties recommended due to short rains

soil.type: Clay-loam soil that retains moisture well, slightly heavier than maize soil
soil.testing_tip: Squeeze a moist handful of soil - if it forms a ball that holds together but crumbles with light pressure, it has good clay content for rice. Pure sand won't hold water long enough.

fertilizer.basal: NPK 15:15:15 at 100-150kg per hectare at planting
fertilizer.topdress: Urea split into two applications - 30 days and 50 days after planting, total around 50-80kg per hectare
fertilizer.organic_alternative: Compost or rice husk ash worked into soil before planting improves both nutrients and water retention

watering.frequency: Upland rice depends on consistent rainfall; supplement with irrigation if a dry spell exceeds 7 days
watering.critical_stages: Flowering and grain-filling stages are most sensitive - water shortage here causes empty grains (panicle sterility)

common_pests_diseases.name: Rice blast
common_pests_diseases.remedy: Use resistant varieties, avoid excess nitrogen, ensure good field drainage

common_pests_diseases.name: Birds
common_pests_diseases.remedy: Use scarecrows, netting, or noise deterrents near harvest

common_pests_diseases.name: Stem borer
common_pests_diseases.remedy: Remove and burn affected tillers, practice field sanitation after harvest

harvest_timeline.signs_of_readiness: 80% of the panicle turns golden yellow, grains hard not milky
harvest_timeline.harvest_method: Cut with sickle close to the ground, sun-dry on tarpaulins for 2-3 days before threshing

beginner_tips: Soak seeds in water for 24 hours before planting to speed up germination
Weeding in the first 6 weeks is critical - rice is a poor competitor against weeds early on
Don't plant rice in pure sandy soil without amendment, it drains too fast and the crop will suffer drought stress

crops.name: Sorghum (Guinea Corn)

forest.notes: Less common in forest zone but grows with sufficient rain

savanna.notes: Major cereal crop here, plant with main rains

sahel.notes: Excellent drought tolerance makes it a staple here

soil.type: Tolerates a wide range including poor sandy soils, but performs best on loamy soils
soil.testing_tip: Sorghum is forgiving of soil quality - if maize struggles on your land, sorghum is a safer bet. Still avoid waterlogged areas.

fertilizer.basal: NPK 15:15:15 at 50-100kg per hectare at planting
fertilizer.topdress: Urea at 25-50kg per hectare about 5 weeks after planting
fertilizer.organic_alternative: Manure application before planting, sorghum responds well even on low-fertility soils

watering.frequency: Largely rain-fed, one of the most drought-tolerant cereals
watering.critical_stages: Flowering stage is most sensitive to drought, though sorghum recovers better than maize from water stress

common_pests_diseases.name: Birds (especially quelea)
common_pests_diseases.sign: Grain loss at the grain-filling/maturity stage
common_pests_diseases.remedy: Bird scaring, netting, harvesting promptly at maturity

common_pests_diseases.name: Striga (witchweed)
common_pests_diseases.sign: Parasitic weed attached to roots, stunted crop
common_pests_diseases.remedy: Crop rotation with legumes, use of resistant varieties, hand-pulling before flowering

common_pests_diseases.name: Smut
common_pests_diseases.sign: Grain heads replaced by black powdery masses
common_pests_diseases.remedy: Use certified disease-free seed, treat seed before planting

harvest_timeline.signs_of_readiness: Grain heads turn brown and hard, lower leaves dry up
harvest_timeline.harvest_method: Cut grain heads with a knife, dry thoroughly before threshing by beating

beginner_tips: If your soil is poor or you're in a drier area, sorghum is more forgiving than maize
Watch for Striga weed - if it appears, don't plant cereals on that plot for a season, rotate with cowpea or groundnut
Birds can wipe out a crop in days at maturity - plan to harvest as soon as grain hardens

crops.name: Pearl Millet

savanna.notes: Grown alongside sorghum in drier savanna areas

sahel.notes: The most drought and heat tolerant major cereal, ideal for this zone

soil.type: Performs well on poor, sandy soils where other cereals fail
soil.testing_tip: If your land is sandy and dries out quickly, millet is one of the few cereals that will still give a reasonable yield

fertilizer.basal: NPK 15:15:15 at 50kg per hectare, millet doesn't need heavy feeding
fertilizer.topdress: Urea at 25kg per hectare 4-5 weeks after planting if rains are good
fertilizer.organic_alternative: Light application of manure, especially useful on very sandy soils to improve water retention

watering.frequency: Almost entirely rain-fed, extremely drought tolerant once established
watering.critical_stages: Germination and early seedling stage need some moisture; once established it survives long dry spells

common_pests_diseases.name: Downy mildew
common_pests_diseases.sign: White/grey fungal growth on leaves, distorted heads
common_pests_diseases.remedy: Use treated seed, remove and destroy infected plants early

common_pests_diseases.name: Head smut
common_pests_diseases.sign: Grain heads replaced with black spore masses
common_pests_diseases.remedy: Seed treatment before planting, crop rotation

common_pests_diseases.name: Birds
common_pests_diseases.remedy: Same as sorghum - scaring, netting, timely harvest

harvest_timeline.days_to_maturity: 75-100 days, one of the fastest cereals
harvest_timeline.signs_of_readiness: Grain heads droop and turn brown, grains feel hard
harvest_timeline.harvest_method: Cut heads by hand, dry on mats, thresh by beating with sticks

beginner_tips: Best choice if your area gets unreliable or short rains - it matures fast and tolerates dry conditions
Thin seedlings to 2-3 per hole after 2 weeks to avoid overcrowding
Often intercropped with cowpea - the cowpea covers the ground and reduces weeds while millet grows tall
